page: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing dedicated Intel Xeon 8252C processors running at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 32.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, relying instead on a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for EBS and network connectivity.

Performance benchmarks highlight strong capabilities in memory-intensive operations and specific processing tasks. The instance achieves top-tier results in large-block memory bandwidth, with read-only speeds of 209.78 GB/sec and write speeds of 98.02 GB/sec. It also demonstrates strong performance in single-threaded gzip compression and software compilation. Standard CPU benchmarks, such as stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core, fall within the average range. However, LLM text generation speeds are relatively weak, reflecting the lack of hardware acceleration for AI workloads.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and high-frequency Intel processors, the x2iezn.12xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring massive memory footprints alongside fast single-core execution. The absence of local storage means storage costs are externalized to network-attached volumes, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This configuration is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, electronic design automation, memory-intensive enterprise applications, and heavy compilation tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by dedicated 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8252C processors. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 1536.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing an exceptional 32.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a 100 Gbps baseline network interface for external storage and connectivity. Benchmark data shows strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth and software compilation, alongside average multi-core CPU performance. This profile makes the x2iezn.12xlarge highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, enterprise applications, and electronic design automation, where massive memory capacity and high CPU clock speeds are critical.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, designed for high-performance in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 1.5 TB of DDR4 RAM, and a 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-intensive virtual server featuring massive DDR4 capacity and high-speed network connectivity for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8252C processors.
1536.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 32.0 GB of RAM per physical core.
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS and network-attached storage.
Strong benchmark performance in large-block memory bandwidth and software compilation.
Best suited for in-memory databases, electronic design automation, and memory-heavy enterprise applications.
